title: Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple
tagline: Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Thripuliyoor Mahavishnu Temple (also called Mayapiran temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu and located in Puliyoor, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India.
overview.description: The Thripuliyoor Mahavishnu Temple (also called Mayapiran temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu and located in Puliyoor, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Mayapiran/Thripuliyoorappan. The nearest railway station to the temple is located at Chengannur, while the nearest airport is Trivandrum International airport. It is

topPlaces.name: Puliyoor
topPlaces.description: Puliyoor is a village in Chengannur municipality in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. The schools in the village include Government HS Puliyoor, Govt.

topPlaces.name: Sree Narayanapuram Thrikkayil Temple
topPlaces.description: Sree Narayanapuram Thrikkayil Temple (Malayalam: ശ്രീ നാരായണപുരം തൃക്കയിൽ ക്ഷേത്രം) is one of the oldest temples in Perissery. The divine idol installed here represents the world–enchanting form of Krishna endowed with the four lustrous arms carrying the conch, the discus, the mace, and the lotus.

topPlaces.name: Perissery
topPlaces.description: Perissery is a village situated 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) west of Chengannur Railway Station in Alappuzha district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Ala, Alappuzha
topPlaces.description: Ala is a village in Chenganoor taluk of Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Budhanoor
topPlaces.description: Budhanoor is a village in Chengannur taluk, in the Alappuzha District of Kerala state, India. It has 14 wards – Kadamboor, Budhanoor West, Budhanoor East, Budhanoor South, Ennakkadu, Ennakkadu North, Ulunthy East, Ulunthy, Elanjimel.

topPlaces.name: Karingattukavu Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: The Karingattukavu Devi Temple is located at Prayar village, Chengannur taluk, Kerala, in India on the bank of the river Pampa. It is a Durga and Bhadrakali temple.

topPlaces.name: Old Syrian Church, Chengannur
topPlaces.description: Pazhaya Suriyani Pally a.k.a. Old Syrian Church, Chengannur is an ancient Syrian Christian church of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple
topPlaces.description: The Thrichittatt Mahavishnu Temple (also called Thiruchenkundrur and Imayavarappan temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Chengannur, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, by Nammalvar, one of the Sri Vaishnava saint-poets of the 8th century called the Alvars.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (97 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (81 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Puliyur Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Puliyoor, Sree Narayanapuram Thrikkayil Temple, Perissery, Ala, Alappuzha, Budhanoor.
